title: cinnamomum tetragonum
intro: Cinnamomum tetragonum, also known as West Indian cinnamon or white cinnamon, is a plant species in the family Lauraceae. It is a type of evergreen tree native to Sri Lanka, commonly used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cinnamomum tetragonum include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as colds and coughs. Cinnamomum tetragonum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used as a spice in cooking, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine and culinary practices.
